Build me an app where I can do push-ups and then my app will count those push-ups. I don't know if augmented reality would be a technology that we need to add here. It basically needs to use a video camera, maybe the user's phone most likely, or plug-ins or whatever. It would basically just use that user's phone. Then, every push-up they do, it would detect each completed push-up, list those on the screen, and then eventually the user could just go back to the app and hit complete or something. It could then log how many push-ups they've done, maybe a day by day thing.

I'm wondering; I feel like we should have a flip view camera, because maybe if there's a selfie view, if the user wants to do more of a selfie view, to ensure that they can see what it's capturing. I feel like that would be the really important camera view. Build this.

userNotes: I hit the start workout button within the app, and it kind of just freezes. I then checked the Xcode logs, and this is what I'm seeing. This is why I'm giving the function a two, mainly because it's broken. I would assume it maybe has potential, but it's just not.  It's the whole core of the app. It can open a camera and then tell the user how many pushups they've done, so it's a pretty big deal if it's not going to work. The app issue is -- This app has crashed because it attempted to access privacy-sensitive data without a usage description.  The app's Info.plist must contain an NSCameraUsageDescription key with a string value explaining to the user how the app uses this data.
prompt:
Create an app where I can do push ups and then my app will count each push up that I do. I don't know if augmented reality is needed for this, but if it is, then definitely implement it. Basically, we'll just look at my push ups and then count them as I complete them. It'll then display them live on the screen, more so like a FaceTime appearance. Mostly video, but maybe just a number of push ups. It's mostly a camera view, but then a number on the screen. The main focus would be the camera view.

It should be counting push ups as I'm doing them, and then it'll also have a way so that I can stop the workout. It would just give me a screen that would summarize how many push ups I've done. You can be creative. Maybe it would tell me how many calories I've burned by using the number of push ups and then running some formula. It should maybe have a way to look at the previous days, kind of like a log, but you can use more formal terminology for the user. More like a recap of different days or by week or something. Build this.

It should be counting push ups as I'm doing them, and then it'll also have a way so that I can stop the workout. It would just give me a screen that would summarize how many push ups I've done. You can be creative. Maybe it would tell me how many calories I've burned by using the number of push ups and then running some formula. It should maybe have a way to look at the previous days, kind of like a log, but you can use more formal terminology for the user. More like a recap of different days or by week or something. Build this.

Build a 5-screen onboarding with progress. Horizontal paging with page indicator dots and progress bar. Each page: illustration placeholder, title, subtitle. Page 3: permission prompt (notifications toggle). Page 4: preference picker (choose interests from a grid). Last page: Get Started and Skip buttons. Animated transitions between pages. Remember completion state in UserDefaults so onboarding only shows once.

Build a todo app with categories. Main screen: segmented control to filter (All, Active, Completed). Sort by due date or priority. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults. Show completion percentage at top.

Build a todo app with categories. Main screen: segmented control to filter (All, Active, Completed). Build an expense tracker with categories. Main screen: list of expenses (amount, label, category icon, date) with running total at top. Add expense sheet: amount field, label, category picker (Food, Transport, Entertainment, etc.), date picker. Filter by category using segmented control. Monthly summary view showing total per category. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a Pomodoro timer with session tracking. Main screen: circular timer with work/break phases. Configurable: work duration (15–60 min), short break (3–10 min), long break (15–30 min), sessions before long break. Start/Pause/Skip controls. Session counter (e.g. 3/4). Today’s stats: completed pomodoros, total focus time. History tab: daily summaries for past 7 days. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a 3-tab app with proper navigation. Tab 1 (Home): dashboard with summary cards. Tab 2 (Browse): searchable list with NavigationStack to detail screens. Tab 3 (Profile): profile view with settings. Badge on Browse tab showing item count. Proper back navigation within each tab. Consistent styling across tabs. Persist minimal state with UserDefaults.

Build a personal finance dashboard with Swift Charts. One screen: a bar chart showing income vs spending for the last 7 days (use sample data like 3 income and 4 spending values). Below the chart, a list of recent transactions (title and amount, positive green and negative red). At the top, a savings goal progress view (e.g. "$200 / $1000"). Use SwiftUI Chart for the bar chart. Keep data in memory or a simple model.

Page 3: permission prompt (notifications toggle). Page 4: preference picker (choose interests from a grid). Last page: Get Started and Skip buttons. Animated transitions between pages. Build a camera app placeholder. Main screen: viewfinder area (dark rectangle) with capture button, flash toggle, and camera flip button. Gallery strip at bottom showing last 3 captures (colored placeholders). Capture creates a new placeholder with timestamp. Gallery screen: grid of all captures with date sections. Detail view: full-screen with share, delete, and info (date, size placeholder). Filters preview strip (Original, B&W, Sepia, Vivid).

Build a habit tracker with Liquid Glass design. One screen: a list of habits (e.g. Exercise, Read, Meditate). Each habit is a card showing its name, current streak count, and a "Check in" button. Use iOS 26 and .glassEffect() on the cards. When the user taps Check in, animate the update and increment the streak. Store streaks in memory or UserDefaults. Include an option to add a new habit.
Build a dice roller app. One screen: a single die icon and a Roll button. Show result 1–6. Optional: second die and sum. Fun, minimal.

An app where you describe what kind of music you’re feeling and it pairs with Apple Music library to give you a curated playlist

It would maybe ask 2-3 critical questions like how many minutes do you have, so it can figure out how many songs to add in the playlist

Build me an app where it tracks my pushups, it would use the phones camera and it needs to have the default be the selfie mode, with a way for the user to flip cameras from the front camera to the back camera. it should have permissions implemented if needed, like it maybe says allow app to use camera, if that's necessary. It should mainly be a camera screen like facetime, but have a number of pushups number that's easy to read from say 6-8 feet away while i'm doing the pushups. the counter needs to update live and it should be looking at my motion to detect when a pushup is done. be creative as well and add in a few more screens that would be useful for the user. maybe one screen shows the history of how many pushups they did by day, maybe goals, how close they are to hitting their goals like a little bar thing that shows how much was completed. like if a user wants 20 pushups in a day and they did 15 that day, it would fill the par 75% to reflect this.
test

It should mainly be a camera screen like facetime, but have a number of pushups number that's easy to read from say 6-8 feet away while i'm doing the pushups. the counter needs to update live and it should be looking at my motion to detect when a pushup is done. be creative as well and add in a few more screens that would be useful for the user. maybe one screen shows the history of how many pushups they did by day, maybe goals, how close they are to hitting their goals like a little bar thing that shows how much was completed. like if a user wants 20 pushups in a day and they did 15 that day, it would fill the par 75% to reflect this.

I am now seeing where it'll say "body detected" or "body not detected". That's a nice little improvement, but I am doing the push-ups and it's not counting. We need to figure out what needs to happen so that, when I do a push-up, the app is then detecting that and then pretty much counting live on the screen. You don't have to change any of the buttons or anything really. Keep the design and everything the same. I just need you to improve that functionality so we can get it to count the push-ups.

Build me an app where it tracks my pushups, it would use the phones camera and it needs to have the default be the selfie mode, with a way for the user to flip cameras from the front camera to the back camera. it should have permissions implemented if needed, like it maybe says allow app to use camera, if that's necessary. It should mainly be a camera screen like facetime, but have a number of pushups number that's easy to read from say 6-8 feet away while i'm doing the pushups. the counter needs to update live and it should be looking at my motion to detect when a pushup is done. be creative as well and add in a few more screens that would be useful for the user. maybe one screen shows the history of how many pushups they did by day, maybe goals, how close they are to hitting their goals like a little bar thing that shows how much was completed. like if a user wants 20 pushups in a day and they did 15 that day, it would fill the par 75% to reflect this.

The pushup counter is double-counting -- it counts 2 for every 1 pushup. Fix the rep counting so one full down-up cycle equals exactly one rep. Use a 2-state machine (idle and wentDown), a smoothing alpha of 0.2, a cooldown of at least 1.0 second, and a hysteresis gap of at least 0.25 between the down threshold (0.60) and up threshold (0.35)."

It should mainly be a camera screen like facetime, but have a number of pushups number that's easy to read from say 6-8 feet away while i'm doing the pushups. the counter needs to update live and it should be looking at my motion to detect when a pushup is done. be creative as well and add in a few more screens that would be useful for the user. maybe one screen shows the history of how many pushups they did by day, maybe goals, how close they are to hitting their goals like a little bar thing that shows how much was completed. like if a user wants 20 pushups in a day and they did 15 that day, it would fill the par 75% to reflect this.

Couples Receipts

Build me an app where it logs different things for a couple. It would basically have an option where a user can upload a receipt and then our app can look at the receipt and itemize it digitally. For example if there is a loaf of bread on the receipt, it will get the entire wording of the item and then it should let the user say whose item it was. If they want to do a certain split percentage-wise, there should be a way for a couple to share access, like a dual account. Somehow we would need a good way to implement that, maybe a partner code or some code that connects the accounts.

I would also need to have a summary of who owes who what. For example if person A is owed $100 and person B is owed $20, it would figure out the net amount so it is just one easy number. There also needs to be a payments tab so each payment would then instantly affect the balance. For example if person A is owed $80 and person B makes the payment of $80, the balance needs to reflect that so it would be a zero balance. 

I also want this to be added as well. Basically there should be another section where if person A owes person B an amount of money (we'll say $1,000) it should log that amount and there should be a separate payments thing for this. Specifically it would be separate from the day-to-day transactions. The day-to-day transactions might be:
* groceries
* gas
* restaurants
* things that one person in the couple might pay for the other person and then they need reimbursement
Whereas there's more of a debt section. I don't know if that's going to be the right name for this second section but we'll just say debts for now. The debt section should have a master balance and then its own unique payments thing as well. If a user makes a payment it's only going to touch the payments for this debt section. That payment would not touch the amount owed on the other component if that makes sense.

projectName: Polished Barcode And QR Code Scanner
prompt: Detect barcodes/QRs from images (still frames) rather than live metadata scanning.

projectName: Polished Barcode And QR Code Scanner
prompt: Update barcode decoding so it shows BOTH raw and normalized values. Add carrier-aware normalization candidates: UPS 1Z (first 18), USPS 22 digits, FedEx 12/15/20 digits, plus suffix candidates (last 22/20/15/12) when the payload has extra prefixes. Copy should default to the normalized primary value, with an option to copy raw.

Persist favorites with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a camera app placeholder. Main screen: viewfinder area (dark rectangle) with capture button, flash toggle, and camera flip button. Gallery strip at bottom showing last 3 captures (colored placeholders). Capture creates a new placeholder with timestamp. Gallery screen: grid of all captures with date sections. Detail view: full-screen with share, delete, and info (date, size placeholder). Filters preview strip (Original, B&W, Sepia, Vivid).

Build a habit tracker with Liquid Glass design. One screen: a list of habits (e.g. Exercise, Read, Meditate). Each habit is a card showing its name, current streak count, and a "Check in" button. Use iOS 26 and .glassEffect() on the cards. When the user taps Check in, animate the update and increment the streak. Store streaks in memory or UserDefaults. Include an option to add a new habit.

Build a quick links app. List of saved links with titles; tap opens a second screen with WKWebView (UIViewRepresentable) loading that URL. Nav bar with back. 2–3 default links. Store in UserDefaults; allow adding link (title + URL).
Build a todo list app. One screen: text field to add a task, a list of tasks with delete, and persist the list in UserDefaults.
Build a notes app. One screen: list of note titles; tap to see/edit one note. Add and delete notes. Persist in UserDefaults.
Build a mood tracker. One screen: pick today’s mood (e.g. 5 emoji buttons), save with date, show last 7 entries in a list. Persist in UserDefaults.
Build a habit checklist for today. One screen: 3–5 habits with checkboxes. Tapping toggles; persist state in UserDefaults. Reset daily or keep history.
Build a minimal expense log. One screen: add amount and label; list recent entries. Persist in UserDefaults. Simple.
Build a countdown timer app. One screen: pick minutes, Start button, and a large countdown display. When it hits zero, show an alert. Simple and clear.
Build a stopwatch app. Start, Stop, Reset, and a lap list. Show elapsed time in large type. Minimal layout.
Build a Pomodoro timer. One screen: 25-minute work timer, 5-minute break. Start/Pause and a simple state label. Minimal.
Build a filterable list. One screen: search bar and a list of 10 items; typing filters the list. Simple.
Build a tab bar app. Two tabs; first shows a list, second shows a placeholder view. Standard tab bar.
Build a list-to-detail app. One screen: list of 5 items; tap to push detail with title and body. NavigationStack.
Build a grid app. One screen: 6 items in a 2-column grid. Each cell shows a number or icon. Tapping shows alert.
Build a pull-to-refresh list. One screen: list of 5 items; pull to refresh shows spinner then “Updated.”
Build a feedback form. One screen: text field and a Submit button. Submit shows a thank-you alert. No backend.
Build a picker screen. One screen: picker with 5 options; show selected below. Single picker.
Build a slider screen. One screen: slider 0–100 and show value. Persist value in UserDefaults.
Build a toggle list. One screen: 3 rows with toggles (e.g. Option A, B, C). Persist in UserDefaults.
Build a color picker app. One screen: three sliders (R, G, B), a color preview, and the hex code below. Tapping hex copies to clipboard. Clean layout.
Build a dice roller app. One screen: a single die icon and a Roll button. Show result 1–6. Optional: second die and sum. Fun, minimal.
Build a tip calculator. Input: bill amount and tip %. Show tip and total. Optional: split among N people. One screen.
Build a BMI calculator. Input height and weight; show BMI and a simple category (e.g. Normal). One screen.
Build a 3-screen onboarding flow. Swipe or Next; last screen has Get Started. No persistence.
Build a modal screen. One screen: button that presents a sheet with “Close” that dismisses. Simple content in sheet.
Build a dark mode aware screen. One screen: background and text that adapt to light/dark. One label and one button.
Build a share sheet screen. One screen: one button “Share”; tap to present share sheet with fixed text “Hello from Vibetree.”
Build a skeleton loading list. One screen: 5 rows that look like skeleton placeholders (gray rectangles); after 2 seconds replace with real text.
Build a card with shadow. One screen: one card (rounded rect) with shadow and a title inside. Clean, elevated look.
Build a gradient background app. One screen: gradient as background (e.g. purple to blue) and one centered title. Minimal.
Build a flashcard app. One screen: one card (question on front); tap to flip and show answer. Next/Previous. 5 fixed cards for MVP.

Build a pixel-perfect clone of the iOS Settings app structure. Use Form with Section headers, NavigationStack for drill-down, Toggle for booleans, Picker for enums, and .listStyle(.insetGrouped). Include at least 5 sections: General (About, Software Update), Display & Brightness (dark mode toggle, text size slider), Sounds (volume slider, toggles), Notifications (per-app toggles), and Privacy (location, contacts toggles). Each section should drill into a detail screen. Use semantic colors throughout — no hardcoded colors. Support Dynamic Type and dark mode.

Build a beautiful 4-step onboarding flow for a fictional wellness app. Step 1: Welcome with animated gradient background and large hero text. Step 2: Feature highlights with 3 cards that animate in with staggered spring animations. Step 3: Notification permission request with a clear value proposition and SF Symbol illustration. Step 4: Name input with Form styling and a primary CTA. Include a page indicator, skip button, and smooth TabView-based transitions. Use .spring() animations, semantic colors, and ensure full dark mode support.

Build a polished contact form with fields for name, email, phone, subject (Picker), and message (TextEditor). Implement inline validation: red caption text below invalid fields (empty name, invalid email format, message too short). Show a character counter for the message field. The Submit button should be .borderedProminent, disabled until all fields are valid, and show a ProgressView while 'sending' (simulated 2-second delay). On success, show an inline checkmark animation — not an alert. Every field must have proper .textContentType and .keyboardType. Add .accessibilityLabel to all controls.

Build an analytics dashboard with a polished card-based layout. Top row: 3 metric cards (total users, revenue, conversion rate) with large .largeTitle numbers, trend arrows (green up / red down SF Symbols), and .caption comparison text. Middle: a line chart (Swift Charts) showing 30 days of data. Bottom: a List of recent events with swipe actions. Include 3 states: loading (skeleton shimmer using .redacted), empty (ContentUnavailableView), and populated. Use .cardStyle() with shadows, 16pt padding, and rounded corners. Pull-to-refresh on the whole ScrollView. Full dark mode and Dynamic Type support.

Build a notes app demonstrating HIG navigation patterns. Sidebar/list: NavigationStack with .searchable, sorted by date. Each row shows title (.headline), preview (.subheadline, .secondary), and date (.caption). Swipe-to-delete with .confirmationDialog confirmation. Detail view: editable TextEditor with .navigationTitle set to the note title. Toolbar: share button, delete button (with destructive alert confirmation), and a 'New Note' button. Empty state: ContentUnavailableView with pencil.line SF Symbol. Persist with UserDefaults + Codable. Animate list insertions with .spring(). Dark mode and accessibility labels throughout.

Build a todo app with categories. Main screen: segmented control to filter (All, Active, Completed). List of tasks with checkboxes, due dates, and priority colors (high=red, medium=orange, low=green). Add task sheet: title, due date picker, priority picker, optional notes. Swipe to delete. Sort by due date or priority. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults. Show completion percentage at top.

Build an expense tracker with categories. Main screen: list of expenses (amount, label, category icon, date) with running total at top. Add expense sheet: amount field, label, category picker (Food, Transport, Entertainment, etc.), date picker. Filter by category using segmented control. Monthly summary view showing total per category. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a rating tracker with categories. Main screen: list of rated items grouped by category (Movies, Books, Restaurants). Each shows name and 1–5 star rating. Add item sheet: name, category picker, star rating selector, optional notes. Detail screen: full info with edit capability. Sort by rating or date. Filter by category. Average rating per category shown in section headers. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a recipe book app. Main screen: grid of recipe cards with title and cook time. Tap for detail: title, servings stepper (adjusts ingredient quantities), ingredients list, and step-by-step instructions. Add/edit recipe sheet: title, servings, ingredients (add/remove rows), instructions (add/remove steps), cook time. Search recipes. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a packing list app with templates. Main screen: list of trips (name, date, progress). Tap trip for sectioned checklist (Clothes, Toiletries, Electronics, Documents). Add item to section. Check/uncheck items. Progress bar showing packed percentage. New trip sheet with name, date, and template picker (Weekend, Business, Beach). Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a goals app with progress tracking. Main screen: 3 active goal cards, each with title, target date, progress slider (0–100%), and status color. Tap card for detail: description, milestones checklist, date created, days remaining. Edit goal sheet. Archive completed goals to a history list. Motivational quote at bottom. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a Pomodoro timer with session tracking. Main screen: circular timer with work/break phases. Configurable: work duration (15–60 min), short break (3–10 min), long break (15–30 min), sessions before long break. Start/Pause/Skip controls. Session counter (e.g. 3/4). Today's stats: completed pomodoros, total focus time. History tab: daily summaries for past 7 days. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a 3-tab app with proper navigation. Tab 1 (Home): dashboard with summary cards. Tab 2 (Browse): searchable list with NavigationStack to detail screens. Tab 3 (Profile): profile view with settings. Badge on Browse tab showing item count. Proper back navigation within each tab. Consistent styling across tabs. Persist minimal state with UserDefaults.

Build a product catalog with NavigationStack. Main screen: list of products with image placeholder, name, price, and rating stars. Tap for detail: large image area, name, price, description, rating, and Add to Favorites button. Favorites screen accessible from toolbar. Search and filter by price range. Sort by price or rating. Persist favorites with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a flashcard study app with decks. Main screen: list of decks with card count and mastery percentage. Tap deck to study: card view with 3D flip animation (question → answer). Buttons: Know It, Still Learning, Skip. Progress bar during study session. Results screen: cards mastered vs needs review. Edit deck: add/edit/delete cards. Create new deck sheet with name and color. Import cards from text. Persist with Codable + UserDefaults.

Build a push-up counter that uses the front camera and Vision human body pose estimation. Phone is placed in front of the user. Show a FaceTime-style preview, a huge rep counter readable from 6–8 feet, and a flip-camera button. Count reps by tracking a stable metric (e.g. shoulder/hip vertical position or elbow angle) with smoothing, hysteresis thresholds, and a rep state machine (down -> up + cooldown). Include on-screen guidance if the body isn't fully visible. Add tabs: Session (live), History (by day), Goals (daily target with progress bar). Persist history/goals with Codable + UserDefaults. Include camera permission handling.

Build a squat coach using Vision body pose. Show a live preview, count reps, and provide simple form cues (e.g. 'go lower', 'stand tall') based on hip and knee angles. Include a calibration step and a sensitivity slider. Persist rep history and show a weekly summary.

Build a gesture remote that uses the front camera and Vision hand pose to detect an open palm vs fist gesture. Use this to toggle a simple in-app media playback demo (play/pause a bundled sound). Show live camera preview and a gesture confidence label. Include a tutorial screen explaining lighting and positioning.

Build a blink reminder app that uses the front camera and Vision face landmarks to estimate blink rate. Show a live preview, blink count per minute, and a gentle reminder if blink rate drops below a threshold. Include a calibration step, and a privacy-first explanation. Persist daily blink stats.
Build an AR measuring app using ARKit + RealityKit. Detect planes, tap to place two points, show distance. Include UI to reset and switch units. Add a 'device required' state for simulator.

Build a Song ID app using ShazamKit. One screen: big 'Listen' button, mic permission handling, and results view with song title/artist/artwork if available. Keep a history list. Include notes about simulator limitations.
Build a simple MetalKit shader playground that renders an animated gradient/noise fragment shader. Provide sliders for speed and color. Include an explanation and fallback if Metal isn't available.
Build a photo filter lab: pick a photo, apply Core Image filters (B&W, sepia, bloom, vignette, sharpen) with sliders, and export. Include before/after toggle and presets.

Build a voice journal app that records audio and transcribes to text using Speech framework. Provide mic permission handling, recording UI, live partial transcription, and a saved entries list. Persist entries and optionally keep audio files.

Build a haptic metronome using Core Haptics. Let the user set BPM (40–200) and choose patterns (tap, double, accent). Provide start/stop and a visual beat indicator. Include graceful fallback if haptics aren't supported.
